bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with the worst possible bid for me (all valueC), which gives me very low utility. Since we're at t=0.00, I should open with my ideal bid (all valueA) which maximizes my utility at 1.0. There's no pressure to concede yet, and the opponent's opening is clearly self-serving.

We're very early (t=0.02). The opponent is offering all valueC (minimum utility for me), with a tiny concession on issueD moving to valueB in their second offer. My best bid is all valueA (utility = 1.0). I should stay near my optimal bid since there's plenty of time left. I'll keep all valueA for now as the opponent hasn't shown meaningful concessions yet.

It's very early (t=0.04) and the opponent is making minimal concessions (still mostly valueC across the board, with only small moves on issueD and issueB). My best offer is all valueA (utility = 1.0). I should stay firm near my top utility this early, but I can make a tiny concession on my lowest-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueF at 0.1 each) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede issueD to valueB (which the opponent already conceded to) while keeping high values elsewhere.

We're very early (t=0.06). The opponent is making tiny concessions - they moved issueD to valueB and issueB to valueB, but everything else remains at valueC (worst for me). My top utility is 1.0 (all valueA). I should continue to push close to my best offer but can make a small concession on low-weight issues. I'll concede issueD and issueE to valueB (both weight 0.1) to signal willingness, while holding firm on issueA (weight 0.3), issueB (0.2), issueC (0.2), and issueF (0.1). This keeps my utility high (~0.87) while showing movement.

I'll keep valueA on issueA, issueB, issueC (my top priorities) and concede slightly on lower-weight issues (D, E, F).
